name: Hyaline / Hyaline-1
summary: Nikolaev & Ravindran (PODC 2019 / PLDI 2021) apply distributed reference counting only at reclamation time (not on access), with retired objects threaded into a shared list whose handle pointers carry per-thread reference counts. Reclamation workload self-balances across threads; snapshot-free and transparent — no thread (un)registration. Hyaline-S delivers HP-grade memory bounds with EBR-grade throughput.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Nikolaev & Ravindran's **Hyaline** (PODC 2019 / PLDI 2021) applies distributed reference counting only at *reclamation time* (not on access). Retired objects are threaded into a shared list whose handle pointers carry the per-thread reference counts; readers do not pay any per-access bookkeeping cost.

Reclamation workload **self-balances across threads** — any thread may free, so a slow thread does not starve fast threads of memory. The scheme is *snapshot-free* and *transparent*: no thread (un)registration is required, which makes it well-suited to dynamically-spawned threads (signal handlers, GC mutators, etc.) that conventional EBR/HP cannot easily accommodate.

**Hyaline-S** delivers HP-grade memory bounds with EBR-grade throughput — closing the gap that motivated the entire post-2017 era/interval line. Works on x86, ARM, PPC, MIPS using LL/SC or DWCAS where available.
